subject: Physics
question_text: The law of rectilinear propagation of light (light travels in straight lines) holds well when:
option_a: The wavelength of light is much larger than the obstacle or aperture size
option_b: The wavelength of light is much smaller than the obstacle or aperture size, making diffraction negligible
option_c: Light travels only through a vacuum

subject: Physics
question_text: Fraunhofer (far-field) diffraction is observed when the source and the screen are:
option_a: Both at finite distances from the diffracting obstacle
option_b: Both effectively at infinity from the obstacle (practically achieved using converging lenses to focus the pattern)
option_d: On opposite sides with no obstacle between them
